enrichment.short_description: Cleanvoice is an AI audio/video editor that automatically removes filler words, background noise, mouth sounds, and silences from podcast recordings.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Cleanvoice AI removes filler words, noise, mouth sounds, and silence from podcast audio and video, with transcription and summaries.

features.title: Filler word removal
features.description: Removes "uh", "um", and similar filler words, supporting 20+ languages.

features.title: Background noise removal
features.description: Eliminates traffic, wind, static, and other ambient noise from recordings.

features.title: Mouth sound & breath removal
features.description: Cleans up mouth clicks, breaths, and stutters automatically.

features.title: Silence trimming
features.description: Detects and shortens long dead air in a recording.

features.title: Multitrack editing
features.description: Processes multiple guest tracks in one pass for multi-speaker episodes.

features.title: Transcription & summaries
features.description: Generates automatic transcripts and episode summaries alongside the edited audio.

features.title: Timeline export
features.description: Exports a reference timeline with timestamps for further manual editing in tools like Audacity or Adobe Audition.

features.title: Customizable templates
features.description: Lets users choose which cleanup features run, or save a reusable editing template.

pros: No signup required to try 30 minutes of processing for free
One-time credit packs are valid for 2 years, useful for infrequent podcasters
Exports compatible with Audacity, Adobe Audition, and Reaper
API available, used by 30+ other brands for automated editing
cons: No persistent free plan for ongoing use beyond the initial trial minutes
Pricing is credit/usage-based rather than a flat unlimited tier
Custom/high-volume API access requires contacting sales

faqs.question: Does Cleanvoice offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can process 30 minutes of audio or video for free without a credit card, plus 30 more minutes after signing up.

faqs.question: How is Cleanvoice priced?
faqs.answer: Through one-time credit packs (from $11 for 5 hours) or monthly/yearly subscriptions (from $11/month for 10 hours), billed per hour of processed audio.

faqs.question: What file formats does Cleanvoice support?
faqs.answer: It supports most audio and video formats, including WAV, MP3, M4A, FLAC, and MP4.

faqs.question: Is there an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cleanvoice offers an API that is used by 30+ brands for automated editing.

faqs.question: How long is my data stored?
faqs.answer: Files are retained for 7 days and then permanently deleted; data is stored in the EU and Cleanvoice is ISO 27001 certified.

use_cases.title: Podcast post-production
use_cases.description: Automatically strip filler words, dead air, and background noise from raw recordings before publishing.

use_cases.title: Multi-guest episode editing
use_cases.description: Edit multiple guest tracks simultaneously and export a clean timeline for manual touch-ups.

use_cases.title: Automated editing at scale
use_cases.description: Use the API to build automatic audio cleanup into an existing content pipeline.

features: Credits roll over up to 3x monthly limit
Credits roll over up to 3x monthly limit
Credits roll over up to 3x monthly limit

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Cleanvoice?
paragraphs: Cleanvoice is an AI-powered audio and video editing tool built for podcast production. It automatically removes filler words, background noise, mouth sounds, and long silences from recordings, and adds transcription and episode summaries.
The service is used by more than 15,000 podcasters, and its API is integrated into more than 30 other products for automated post-production.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Cleanvoice is for
paragraphs: Cleanvoice suits podcasters, audio engineers, and video editors who want to cut manual cleanup time, as well as media agencies and platforms that use its API to automate editing for their own users.

enrichment.short_description: Krisp is an AI app that cancels background noise and echo on any calling app and generates meeting transcripts, summaries, and notes.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Krisp removes background noise and echo from calls and meetings, and provides AI transcription, summaries, and action items.

features.title: AI noise cancellation
features.description: Removes background noise, echo, and cross-talk at the audio level, working underneath any calling app.

features.title: AI Note Taker
features.description: Produces meeting transcripts, summaries, and CRM-ready updates.

features.title: Accent conversion
features.description: Converts speaker or listener accents during live calls.

features.title: Custom vocabulary transcription
features.description: Improves transcript accuracy with up to 750 custom terms.

features.title: Action item tracking
features.description: Automatically organizes and tracks meeting action items.

features.title: Call Center AI
features.description: A dedicated edition with voice isolation and after-call summaries for support agents.

pros: Works underneath any calling or conferencing app rather than needing per-app integration
Free tier plus a 7-day free trial of premium Meeting AI features
Supports 16 languages for transcripts and meeting summaries
SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, and PCI-DSS compliance for enterprise buyers
cons: Accent conversion hours and storage are capped by plan (e.g., 1 hr/day and 10GB on Core)
Enterprise features like SSO/SCIM and on-device transcription require custom Enterprise pricing
Advanced integrations like Salesforce require the higher-priced Advanced plan

faqs.question: Is Krisp free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Krisp offers a free tier plus a 7-day free trial of its paid Meeting AI features.

faqs.question: How much does Krisp cost?
faqs.answer: The Core plan is $16/user/month ($8/month billed annually), and Advanced is $30/user/month ($15/month billed annually); Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: What conferencing apps does Krisp work with?
faqs.answer: It works at the audio level with apps including Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Webex, and Discord calls.

faqs.question: Does Krisp support languages other than English?
faqs.answer: Yes, transcripts and meeting summaries are available in 16 languages, including Spanish, French, German, and Korean.

faqs.question: Where is Krisp based?
faqs.answer: Krisp's headquarters is in Berkeley, California.

use_cases.title: Noise-free remote calls
use_cases.description: Remove background noise and echo from any calling app during meetings or customer calls.

use_cases.title: Automated meeting notes
use_cases.description: Generate transcripts, summaries, and CRM-ready notes from meetings automatically.

use_cases.title: Call center audio quality
use_cases.description: Apply voice isolation and after-call summaries to support agent calls.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Krisp?
paragraphs: Krisp is an AI voice and meeting assistant that cancels background noise, echo, and cross-talk on any calling app, and generates meeting transcripts, summaries, and action items.
Beyond individual meetings, Krisp also offers a Call Center AI product with voice isolation and after-call summaries, plus a developer API for noise cancellation and accent conversion.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Krisp is for
paragraphs: Krisp is built for remote workers and sales/support teams who want cleaner call audio, as well as call centers and BPOs that need noise cancellation and analytics across many agents.

enrichment.short_description: Auphonic is a web-based AI audio post-production service that automatically levels, cleans, and normalizes podcast and video audio.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Auphonic automatically levels, cleans, and normalizes podcast and video audio, with AI speech-to-text and multitrack processing.

features.title: Intelligent leveling
features.description: Balances volume between speakers and background music automatically.

features.title: Noise & reverb reduction
features.description: Reduces background noise and room reverb in recordings.

features.title: Filler word & silence removal
features.description: Automatically cuts filler words, coughs, and long silences.

features.title: Multitrack processing
features.description: Applies ducking and noise gates across multiple speaker tracks.

features.title: Loudness normalization
features.description: Normalizes output to broadcast and streaming loudness specifications.

features.title: Speech-to-text & show notes
features.description: Generates transcripts and automatic show notes on paid plans.

features.title: Video support
features.description: Processes video files and can generate chapters and audiograms.

features.title: Publishing integrations
features.description: Connects to YouTube, Libsyn, Podbean, SoundCloud, plus Zapier, API, and CLI access.

pros: Free tier processes 2 hours of audio per month at no cost
One-time credit packages don't expire, useful for infrequent use
Accessible via web app, API, CLI, and Zapier for automation
Cites large publishers such as BBC Radio, Buzzsprout, and iHeartRadio as users
cons: Free tier adds an Auphonic watermark jingle and excludes speech recognition/show notes
Free tier limits multitrack productions to under 20 minutes
Subscription and credit prices are not shown as static figures on the pricing page (rendered dynamically by currency/region)

faqs.question: Is Auphonic free?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free tier processes 2 hours of audio per month, though it adds an Auphonic jingle and excludes speech recognition and show notes.

faqs.question: What does Auphonic do?
faqs.answer: It automatically levels, cleans, and normalizes podcast and video audio, and can generate transcripts and show notes.

faqs.question: Can I buy audio processing credits without a subscription?
faqs.answer: Yes, one-time credit packages are available from 5 hours up to 3000+ hours, and unused credits don't expire.

faqs.question: Does Auphonic support video?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can process video files and generate chapters and audiograms.

faqs.question: What speech recognition does Auphonic use?
faqs.answer: Paid plans include multilingual speech-to-text, and the site references OpenAI's Whisper model.

use_cases.title: Podcast audio cleanup
use_cases.description: Automatically level, denoise, and normalize episode audio before publishing.

use_cases.title: Multitrack interview processing
use_cases.description: Apply ducking and noise gates across multiple speaker tracks in one pass.

use_cases.title: Automated show notes
use_cases.description: Generate transcripts and show notes automatically alongside audio processing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Auphonic?
paragraphs: Auphonic is a web-based AI audio post-production service that automatically levels, cleans, and normalizes speech and music. It handles noise and reverb reduction, filler-word and silence removal, and loudness normalization to broadcast standards.
The service also offers multilingual speech-to-text, automatic show notes, and video support with chapters and audiograms, accessible via its web app, API, CLI, or Zapier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Auphonic is for
paragraphs: Auphonic is aimed at podcasters, audio engineers, and video creators who want consistent, professional-sounding audio without manually mixing every episode.

enrichment.short_description: Resemble AI provides voice cloning and text-to-speech generation alongside AI deepfake detection and audio/video watermarking.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Resemble AI offers enterprise voice cloning and text-to-speech alongside deepfake detection and audio/video watermarking tools.

features.title: Rapid Clone
features.description: Creates a working voice clone in under 1 minute from about 10 seconds of audio.

features.title: Professional Clone
features.description: Higher-fidelity voice clone trained from 10-25+ minutes of audio, with a fuller emotional range.

features.title: Voice Design
features.description: Generates new synthetic voice candidates from a text description of desired characteristics.

features.title: Zero-shot multilingual cloning
features.description: The Chatterbox model clones voices across 23 languages without separate training runs.

features.title: Detect
features.description: Multimodal deepfake detection for audio, image, and video content.

features.title: Verify
features.description: Invisible watermarking for tracking audio/image/video content provenance.

features.title: Resemble Meetings
features.description: A bot that can be invited into live calls to flag synthetic audio in real time.

features.title: Developer access
features.description: REST API, Python and Node.js SDKs, and self-hosted Docker/Kubernetes deployment.

pros: Combines voice generation with deepfake detection and watermarking in one platform
Pay-as-you-go Flex plan requires no upfront commitment and credits don't expire
Supports on-premises/self-hosted deployment for security-sensitive customers
SOC2 Type II, GDPR, and HIPAA compliance claimed for enterprise plans
cons: No published flat monthly pricing; usage is billed per second/search and Enterprise requires contacting sales
Founding year and headquarters are not disclosed on the site
Custom model training and SSO/SAML are reserved for the Enterprise tier

faqs.question: What does Resemble AI do?
faqs.answer: It provides AI voice cloning and text-to-speech generation, plus deepfake detection (Detect) and content watermarking (Verify) for audio, image, and video.

faqs.question: How much does Resemble AI cost?
faqs.answer: The Flex plan starts at $0 and bills usage per second/search (e.g., $0.04/second for audio detection), while Enterprise plans use custom volume pricing.

faqs.question: How many languages does voice cloning support?
faqs.answer: The Chatterbox model supports zero-shot voice cloning across 23 languages.

faqs.question: Can Resemble AI detect AI-generated voices in real time?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resemble Meetings lets users invite a detection bot into live calls to flag synthetic audio.

faqs.question: Is on-premises deployment available?
faqs.answer: Yes, Enterprise plans support on-premises or self-hosted deployment via Docker/Kubernetes.

use_cases.title: Branded voice cloning
use_cases.description: Create a custom cloned voice for products, IVR, or content from a short audio sample.

use_cases.title: Synthetic media detection
use_cases.description: Screen audio, image, or video for AI-generated deepfakes before acting on it.

use_cases.title: Live call protection
use_cases.description: Monitor meetings in real time for synthetic voice impersonation.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Resemble AI?
paragraphs: Resemble AI is a voice AI platform that combines generative voice cloning and text-to-speech with deepfake detection and watermarking. Its Rapid Clone and Professional Clone tools generate custom voices from short audio samples, while Detect and Verify screen and mark synthetic audio, image, and video content.
The company's clients span telecom, finance, media, and public-sector organizations, including Netflix, Paramount, and Deutsche Telekom.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Resemble AI is for
paragraphs: Resemble AI suits enterprises that need both branded voice generation and protection against voice/video deepfakes, such as media companies, call centers, and security teams handling executive-impersonation risk.

name: PlayHT
skip_reason: Official site (play.ht) unreachable — DNS resolution fails for the play.ht domain from this research environment (confirmed via WebFetch and independent curl/nslookup checks), so facts could not be verified directly from the primary source.

enrichment.short_description: LOVO (Genny) is an AI text-to-speech and video editing platform offering 500+ voices, voice cloning, and auto-generated subtitles.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LOVO (Genny) offers 500+ AI voices in 100+ languages for text-to-speech, voice cloning, and video editing with auto-subtitles.

features.title: Text-to-speech
features.description: 500+ voices across 100+ languages and accents.

features.title: Voice cloning
features.description: Creates a custom voice clone from about one minute of reference audio.

features.title: Video editor
features.description: Built-in online editor with audio-video synchronization.

features.title: Auto subtitle generator
features.description: Generates subtitles supporting 20+ languages.

features.title: AI Writer
features.description: Generates scripts for voiceover and video content.

features.title: AI art generator
features.description: Creates royalty-free images for use in projects.

features.title: Team workspaces
features.description: Cloud storage and shared team workspaces.

features.title: Developer API
features.description: API access for integrating voice generation into other products.

pros: Broad voice and language coverage (500+ voices, 100+ languages)
Combines TTS, video editing, scriptwriting, and image generation in one tool
14-day free trial of the Pro plan with no credit card required
Claims a large global user base (2M+ users)
cons: No specific pricing plans or dollar amounts are published on the site; users must start a trial or contact sales
No documented free-forever plan, only a time-limited trial
Team collaboration and API access are marked as newer additions with limited public detail

faqs.question: Does LOVO offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day trial of the Pro plan is available with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How many voices and languages does LOVO support?
faqs.answer: LOVO offers over 500 voices across more than 100 languages and accents.

faqs.question: Can I clone my own voice with LOVO?
faqs.answer: Yes, Genny can create a custom voice clone from about one minute of audio.

faqs.question: Where is LOVO based?
faqs.answer: LOVO's office is listed at SkyDeck, 2150 Shattuck Ave, Berkeley, California.

faqs.question: Does LOVO have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, an API is available for developers to integrate LOVO's voice generation.

use_cases.title: Marketing and social videos
use_cases.description: Add AI voiceover and auto-subtitles to short-form video content.

use_cases.title: E-learning and training narration
use_cases.description: Generate multilingual voiceovers for corporate training and courses.

use_cases.title: Custom brand voice
use_cases.description: Clone a consistent voice for ongoing video or IVR content.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LOVO?
paragraphs: LOVO is an AI voice generation and video editing platform built around its Genny studio. It provides text-to-speech in 500+ voices across 100+ languages, voice cloning from a short audio sample, and an integrated video editor with auto-generated subtitles.
The platform also includes an AI scriptwriting tool and royalty-free AI image generation, aimed at creators producing marketing, training, and social content.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LOVO is for
paragraphs: LOVO is aimed at marketers, e-learning teams, and content creators who need multilingual voiceovers and quick video editing without hiring voice actors.

enrichment.short_description: WellSaid is an AI text-to-speech studio with 120+ voices built from licensed voice-actor recordings, with commercial usage rights on paid plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: WellSaid is an AI text-to-speech studio with 120+ voices, commercial usage rights, and tone/pacing controls for training and marketing videos.

features.title: 120+ AI voices
features.description: Voices built from licensed voice-actor recordings rather than scraped audio.

features.title: AI Director
features.description: Adjusts pronunciation, pacing, and tone of generated speech.

features.title: Pronunciation library
features.description: Includes Oxford Dictionary integration for consistent pronunciation.

features.title: Team workspaces
features.description: Shared projects and commenting for collaborating on scripts.

features.title: Adobe integrations
features.description: Integrates with Adobe Express and Adobe Premiere Pro.

features.title: Developer API
features.description: Programmatic access for LMS, IVR, and app integrations.

features.title: Commercial usage rights
features.description: Included on all paid plans for generated audio.

features.title: Enterprise access controls
features.description: Role-based access control and SSO available on Enterprise.

pros: Voices are built from licensed voice-actor recordings rather than scraped audio
Commercial rights and unlimited generation included on every paid plan
Free trial available with no credit card required
SOC2 and GDPR compliance for enterprise buyers
cons: Free trial is limited to 3 downloaded minutes and 10 minutes of generation, with no commercial rights
Non-English languages beyond German and French require the Enterprise plan
Higher-resolution audio exports (48-96 kHz) and extra formats require Pro tier or above

faqs.question: Is WellSaid free to try?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial offers 10 minutes of generation and 3 downloaded minutes per month, though without commercial usage rights.

faqs.question: How much does WellSaid cost?
faqs.answer: Starter is $10/month billed annually ($19/month billed monthly); Pro is $33/month billed annually ($49/month monthly); Business and Enterprise are custom/team-priced.

faqs.question: Does WellSaid include commercial usage rights?
faqs.answer: Yes, all paid plans include full commercial rights to generated audio.

faqs.question: What editing tools integrate with WellSaid?
faqs.answer: Adobe Express and Adobe Premiere Pro integrations are available, along with a developer API.

faqs.question: Did WellSaid Labs change its domain?
faqs.answer: Yes, wellsaidlabs.com now redirects to wellsaid.io, the current site for the same product.

use_cases.title: Corporate training narration
use_cases.description: Generate consistent AI voiceovers for training and e-learning content.

use_cases.title: Marketing video voiceover
use_cases.description: Produce branded voiceovers for product marketing and campaigns.

use_cases.title: Team voice production
use_cases.description: Collaborate on scripts and shared voice projects across a team workspace.
use_cases.audience: Content and video teams at larger companies

long_form_blocks.heading: What is WellSaid?
paragraphs: WellSaid (formerly WellSaid Labs) is an AI text-to-speech studio offering more than 120 voices built from licensed voice-actor recordings. Its AI Director tool lets users adjust pronunciation, pacing, and tone, and all paid plans include full commercial usage rights.
The company's customers include Microsoft, Adobe, and Amazon, and the platform integrates with Adobe Express and Premiere Pro.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who WellSaid is for
paragraphs: WellSaid is aimed at corporate learning and development, marketing, and video production teams that need commercially licensed voiceovers with tone and pacing control.

enrichment.short_description: Listnr is an AI text-to-speech platform with 1000+ voices across 142+ languages for voiceovers, podcasts, and video content.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Listnr converts text into natural AI voiceovers with 1000+ voices across 142+ languages for podcasts, videos, and e-learning.

features.title: 1000+ TTS voices
features.description: Voices spanning 142+ languages and accents.

features.title: Voice cloning
features.description: Create a custom cloned voice for ongoing content.

features.title: Multi-voice conversations
features.description: Combine multiple voices and languages in a single audio file.

features.title: SSML & pronunciation controls
features.description: Custom pronunciation definitions and SSML tag support.

features.title: Adjustable delivery
features.description: Control pitch, rate, and emphasis before generating final audio.

features.title: Preview mode
features.description: Preview speech before committing to final generation.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects via Zapier and Pabbly Connect, plus API access.

features.title: Commercial usage rights
features.description: Included with generated audio.

pros: Large voice and language library (1,000+ voices, 142+ languages)
Free tier with 1,000 words and no credit card required
Recognized as a Product Hunt #1 Product of the Day and Golden Kitty Award winner
Annual plans include 2 months free versus paying monthly
cons: Paid plans are billed annually up front rather than offering flexible monthly billing
Custom SLAs and quarterly reviews are reserved for the top Agency plan
Video generation minutes and credits are capped per plan tier

faqs.question: Is Listnr free?
faqs.answer: Yes, new users get 1,000 words of text-to-speech generation for free with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Listnr cost?
faqs.answer: Plans are billed annually: Individual at $190/year, Solo at $390/year, and Agency at $990/year.

faqs.question: How many voices and languages does Listnr support?
faqs.answer: Over 1,000 voices across more than 142 languages and accents.

faqs.question: When was Listnr founded?
faqs.answer: Listnr was founded in 2020.

faqs.question: Does Listnr offer an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, API access is available alongside Zapier and Pabbly Connect integrations.

use_cases.title: Podcast and YouTube voiceovers
use_cases.description: Generate natural-sounding narration for podcasts, YouTube videos, and shorts.

use_cases.title: Audiobook narration
use_cases.description: Produce long-form narration for audiobooks using a large voice library.

use_cases.title: E-learning voiceovers
use_cases.description: Create multilingual narration for training and course content.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Listnr?
paragraphs: Listnr is an AI text-to-speech platform offering more than 1,000 voices across 142+ languages, with voice cloning, multi-speaker audio, and SSML controls. Founded in 2020, it serves a claimed user base of 3.5 million people.
Beyond voice generation, Listnr supports commercial usage rights, API access, and integrations with Zapier and Pabbly Connect for automated workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Listnr is for
paragraphs: Listnr is built for podcasters, YouTubers, and e-learning teams who need fast, multilingual voiceovers without hiring voice talent.

name: Podium
skip_reason: Official site (www.podium.page / hello.podium.page) returned repeated 503/404 errors across multiple URL variants during research, and web search results conflate this product with an unrelated same-named company (Podium.com, a business texting/reviews SaaS), making it impossible to verify accurate, product-specific facts.

enrichment.short_description: Rask AI translates and dubs videos into 130+ languages using AI voice cloning, transcription, and lip-sync.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Rask AI translates and dubs videos into 130+ languages with AI voice cloning, transcription, and lip-sync synchronization.

features.title: Video translation & dubbing
features.description: Translates and dubs videos into 130+ languages.

features.title: Voice cloning
features.description: Clones speaker voices in 32 languages for dubbed audio.

features.title: Lip-sync
features.description: Synchronizes lip movement with translated speech.

features.title: Speech-to-text & captions
features.description: Transcribes speech and auto-generates captions/subtitles.

features.title: Multi-speaker translation
features.description: Handles multiple speakers and languages within a single project.

features.title: Translation dictionary
features.description: Custom glossary and prompt-based script adjustment for consistent terminology.

features.title: Built-in editor
features.description: In-browser video editor for reviewing and refining translated output.

features.title: API access
features.description: Automate localization workflows via API.

pros: Free plan available with 3 minutes of processing and no credit card
Broad language coverage for both translation (130+) and voice cloning (32)
Annual billing offers large discounts (up to ~48%) over monthly plans
Unused minutes roll over on annual subscriptions
cons: Free plan is limited to 3 minutes, too small to evaluate longer content
Monthly billing is notably more expensive than annual (e.g., Creator Pro $150/mo vs $78/mo annual)
Enterprise-level SLAs and dedicated API access require custom/contact-sales pricing

faqs.question: Is Rask AI free?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan includes 3 minutes of video localization and voice cloning in 32 languages.

faqs.question: How much does Rask AI cost?
faqs.answer: Creator starts at $33/month billed annually ($60/month monthly); Creator Pro is $78/month annually ($150/month monthly); Business and Enterprise scale up from there.

faqs.question: How many languages does Rask AI support?
faqs.answer: Video translation covers 130+ languages, and voice cloning covers 32 languages.

faqs.question: Does Rask AI sync lips to translated audio?
faqs.answer: Yes, Creator Pro and above include lip-sync to preserve speaker identity in dubbed video.

faqs.question: Where is Rask AI based?
faqs.answer: Its parent company, Brask Inc., lists a registered address in Dover, Delaware, US.

use_cases.title: YouTube/course localization
use_cases.description: Translate and dub existing videos into multiple languages with matching lip movement.

use_cases.title: Marketing video dubbing
use_cases.description: Localize marketing and product videos for international markets.

use_cases.title: Multi-language e-learning
use_cases.description: Produce translated course videos with voice cloning and captions.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Rask AI?
paragraphs: Rask AI is a video and audio localization platform that translates and dubs videos into more than 130 languages using AI voice cloning and lip-sync. It also provides speech-to-text transcription, auto-generated captions, and a translation dictionary for consistent terminology.
The product is part of Brask Inc., and serves creators, marketers, EdTech, and businesses that need to localize video content at scale.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Rask AI is for
paragraphs: Rask AI suits content creators, marketers, and e-learning teams who need to translate and dub video into multiple languages without hiring voice talent or manual lip-sync editors.
